channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Legal experts warn Alberta separation process would create major constitutional and economic uncertainty
item.description: A CTV News report examines the growing debate around Alberta separatism and the legal complications that could arise if the province were to pursue separation from Canada. The article highlights comme...

item.title: Doctors caution about rising online misinformation about menopause
item.description: Health professionals are warning about a rapidly growing wave of online misinformation surrounding menopause, highlighting concerns that inaccurate or misleading content is increasingly shaping how pe...

item.title: Clashes and protests in Spain after police use force on returning Gaza flotilla activists at Bilbao airport
item.description: In Spain, strong public reaction has emerged after clashes between Basque police and activists linked to a Gaza aid flotilla at Bilbao airport. Around two thousand people staged a protest in Bilbao on...

item.title: France promotes STEM education and career opportunities for Nigerian students at France Alumni Day in Lagos
item.description: French government officials and Nigerian alumni have encouraged more Nigerian students to consider France for higher education, especially in Science, Technology, Engineering and Mathematics (STEM)-re...

item.title: Early Beach Photos of Norma Jeane (Marilyn Monroe) With Her Dog Ruffles in 1947
item.description: This article highlights a series of rare vintage photographs taken in 1947 showing Norma Jeane Dougherty, who would later become internationally known as Marilyn Monroe, during a relaxed day at the be...

item.title: Critical review of Errol Flynn biography explores Hollywood fame, racism, and sexual violence allegations
item.description: This article reviews Patricia O’Brien’s biography of Australian Hollywood star Errol Flynn, situating his life within the cultural and racial assumptions of the early to mid-20th century. Flynn, one o...

item.title: How Catholic nuns are navigating social media visibility and historical tensions between autonomy and Church authority
item.description: Catholic nuns and religious sisters are increasingly visible on social media platforms such as TikTok, Instagram and podcasts, where they share insights into their daily routines, beliefs and motivati...

item.title: Maina Kageni settles KSh 1 million bet after Arsenal clinch Premier League title
item.description: Veteran Kenyan radio presenter Maina Kageni has honoured a KSh 1 million bet he made with Nakuru businessman Sam Mburu after Arsenal were officially crowned the 2025/26 English Premier League champion...

item.title: Australian Foreign Minister Penny Wong to attend Quad Foreign Ministers' Meeting in New Delhi
item.description: Australian Foreign Minister Penny Wong has announced her visit to India to participate in the Quad Foreign Ministers' Meeting scheduled in New Delhi on 26 May 2026. During the visit, she will also hol...

item.title: Review of Zayd Ibn Isah’s ‘The Nigerian Spirit Is Special’ and reflections on Nigerian identity
item.description: The article is a reflective literary review of Zayd Ibn Isah’s work, particularly his book ‘The Nigerian Spirit Is Special’, which is a collection of essays exploring Nigerian identity, resilience, an...

item.title: Diet Coke shortages in India linked to aluminium supply delays, while Australian supply remains stable
item.description: India is experiencing a significant shortage of Diet Coke, leading to panic buying, stockpiling, and unusually high prices for the soft drink in some venues. Reports indicate that some pubs and social...

item.title: U.S. and Iran outline framework to extend ceasefire and reopen Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The United States and Iran are reportedly advancing discussions on a memorandum of understanding that would establish a framework to extend their current ceasefire by 60 days. According to a senior ad...

item.title: CHED orders closure of unapproved degree programs at Saint Therese College in Taguig
item.description: The Commission on Higher Education (CHED) has ordered Saint Therese College of Taguig City to immediately stop offering three Bachelor of Science programs after finding that these courses were being r...

item.title: Andy Warhol’s 1982 Polaroids of Jane Fonda and Their Role in His Silkscreen Portraits
item.description: This article explores a series of Polaroid photographs of actress and activist Jane Fonda taken by pop art icon Andy Warhol in 1982 at his New York studio known as The Factory. Warhol frequently used ...

item.title: Key Nigerian Newspaper Headlines for Monday, 25 May 2026
item.description: The Naija News roundup for Monday, 25th May 2026 highlights major developments across Nigerian politics, security, sports, entertainment, and aviation. In politics, President Bola Tinubu has emerged v...

item.title: Bethany Clarke reflects on Laos methanol poisoning that killed her friend
item.description: This report explores the emotional and physical aftermath of a fatal methanol poisoning incident involving travellers in Laos in November 2024. Bethany Clarke, a British backpacker, survived after dri...

item.title: Ozzy Osbourne’s 1984 Public Intoxication Arrest and Iconic Mugshot During the Bark at the Moon Tour
item.description: This article recounts an incident from May 1984 during Ozzy Osbourne’s Bark at the Moon Tour, when the rock musician was arrested for public intoxication in Memphis, Tennessee. After performing a conc...

item.title: Cockroach Janta Party says it plans youth-focused campaigns on unemployment, education and accountability
item.description: The Cockroach Janta Party (CJP), a satirical social media movement started by political communication strategist Abhijeet Dipke, has announced that it intends to evolve into an independent youth-drive...

item.title: Senior Carpenters’ Union Officials Resign Amid Investigation Into Housing Purchases and Governance Concerns
item.description: Senior leadership at the Carpenters’ Regional Council (CRC), one of Canada’s largest construction unions, has resigned amid an internal investigation triggered by reporting on the organisation’s real ...

item.title: Texas GOP lawmaker criticizes Trump-backed Ken Paxton over controversial plea deal in child abuse case
item.description: A Texas Republican state lawmaker has publicly broken with President Donald Trump over his endorsement of Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton in the race for U.S. Senate. State Rep. Jeff Leach, who has ...
